Gardening with Ms. Linda
We worked efficiently to get our warm 
weather crops in the ground before it rained on Thursday. This week we planted peppers next to cucumbers and marigolds and basil next to tomatoes. These companion plants help each other grow, deter pests and even enhance flavor. In addition, they share sunlight, moisture and soil-pH preferences. We constructed three trellises for our snap peas and cucumbers, both vining plants requiring support to maintain proper air circulation.
We noticed that there are many flowers in the blue-violet-purple color range blooming in our garden. Bees are particularly attracted to these nectar-rich blooms. We looked closely at the patterns on flower petals that direct pollinators to the nectar source. Flowers and bees have evolved together for their mutual survival over millions of years.
Spring Fling & Student Art Show — Friday, May 29 | 5–7pm
Come celebrate the end of a wonderful school year! Join us for our Spring Fling and Student Art Show on Friday, May 29 from 5–7pm. It's an evening to honor all of the incredible work our kids have accomplished and to admire the beautiful artwork they've been creating. There will be food, drinks, and entertainment for the whole family — siblings are more than welcome! A suggested donation of $20 per family can be sent via Venmo 
to
@SOCDS-PEP
. We hope to see you there!
Last Three Weeks
We are in the final countdown of the school year.  We have been practicing our clap-out to the song, We Are Family by Sister Sledge (1995 Remix.). At the We Are Family chorus we roll our hands side to side four times, and then we double pump our hands two times, and end with making a rainbow.  We will continue to practice over these last few weeks.
I will send out the statements for the last four week installment this weekend.  Sorry for the delay.
​​If you have any questions or concerns please text (1-646-226-8790) or email learn@socds.com.
Annemarie
Art with Ms. Jenny
Ms. Jenny is in the final stages of preparing for the Art Show.  See you all there.

Logistics
Dress Appropriately for the Weather:
Please dress your child to be comfortable outside for 30 minutes at a time, including solid shoes for running.
Morning Snack
:  Please send in a morning, and if your child is staying for enrichment please send in an afternoon snack as well.  We generally allow children to choose their food and to listen to their bodies, but if you have any particular preference for what they eat and when, please just label the containers and we will accommodate.  Please remember we are a NUT FREE environment.
Water Bottles
:  Please send in a labeled water bottle with your child each day.  We do make sure that they drink 
water throughout the day.  We have two water fountains which we use to refill the bottles (so they may come home full.)
Parking:
Please remember that Vose Avenue is an active busy street.  Please pull up on the
school side
of Vose in front of the school.  It is safest to go around the block instead of turning around in driveways.  For your child's safety please try to have them leave your car from the sidewalk side of the car.
Please do not block our neighbors driveway, even for a few minutes.
Tuition:
As I receive payments I update the statement and send you a copy.  If you do not receive those from me please let me know.
Hours
Morning Care 7:30-8:30am
Morning Drop off is between 8:30-9am
Afternoon Pick Up is between 3:10- 3:20pm
Extended Day 3:20-5:30/5:45pm
South Orange Country Day School
461 Vose Avenue, South Orange
973.762.6451
